name: Coast and Garraf Natural Park with the Gravel bike
name_original: Küste und Naturpark Garraf mit dem Gravelbike
description:
Steep climbs with an ocean panorama, the finest gravel roads, a Buddhist monastery and the famous Bodega Güell de Gaudí await you on the 60-kilometer tour through the Garraf Nature Park. You can take the train from Barcelona Sants train station to Platja de Castelldefels to save yourself the first 20 kilometers and get straight onto the more scenic route. You can also take the tour in the other direction and end on the coast.

From Platja de Castelldefels you first drive along the coast on a paved road, past Garraf and with a wonderful view of the blue sea to the horizon. Now you take the steepest climb of the tour under your tires: On the Camí de la Trinitat, the concrete surface is really steep. With a 14% incline, there's no shame in pushing the bike a bit. At the small chapel you can take a short breather and marvel at the view.

Soon you have conquered the first mountain and you are in the middle of the Parc del Garraf. On quiet gravel roads you roll through the special, gently rolling landscape with the typical Mediterranean thicket. A few kilometers further you will reach the monastery - it is worth taking a break here to admire the stupa and, if you have enough time, to take a guided tour. Or you'll come back another time; meditation courses and retreats are also offered.

For the next ten kilometers you drive on a fantastic gravel route across the nature park and finally reach Begues. You've managed the altitude difference for today - from now on it's only downhill.

After a kilometer-long, fast descent you reach the banks of the Llobregat, along which you drive a little further until you turn your back on nature and head back into the urban realm. Via the Carretera Reial, which later becomes the Avinguda del Paralel, you get back to the city and look back on a long, eventful day in the saddle.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Castelldefels offers diverse landscapes, from coastal plains to challenging mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Garraf Natural Park, featuring rugged limestone hills, pine forests, and scrubland. Riders can expect a mix of rocky, dry trails and more relaxed, often paved, coastal routes. This geographical position provides varied gravel bike trails Castelldefels, catering to different skill levels. Best gravel bike trails around Castelldefels The most popular gravel bike trail is Coast and Garraf Natural Park with the Gravel bike, a 39.0 miles (62.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route combines coastal riding with the rugged interior of Garraf Natural Park. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Llobregat River Footbridge – Llobregat River Park loop from Castelldefels, a moderate 41.4 miles (66.7 km) path. This route features flatter terrain along the Llobregat River, offering a different kind of scenic experience. Local gravel bikers also love the Sakya Tashi Ling Monastery – Castelldefels Beach loop from Castelldefels, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail leading through the Garraf Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Gravel biking around Castelldefels is defined by coastal plains, limestone hills, and pine forests within the Garraf Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate river loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Castelldefels are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Castelldefels's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Castelldefels?
A: Gravel biking around Castelldefels offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find rugged and wild interiors within the &lt;strong&gt;Garraf Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by pine forests, limestone hills, and scrubland, often with rocky and dry trails. Closer to the coast, routes can be flatter, sometimes on mostly paved surfaces, especially along the Llobregat River and towards Platja de Gava. Many routes combine these peri-urban and natural landscapes, providing panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there beginner-friendly gravel routes in Castelldefels?
A: While many routes in the Garraf Natural Park are challenging, there are options suitable for all skill levels. The coastal plains and areas along the Llobregat River offer more relaxed gravel rides, often on mostly paved surfaces. For a moderate experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31357140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat River Footbridge – Llobregat River Park loop from Castelldefels&lt;/a&gt;, which features flatter terrain.

Q: Where can I find challenging gravel bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders seeking a challenge will find plenty within the &lt;strong&gt;Garraf Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e680631082/coast-and-garraf-natural-park-with-the-gravel-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coast and Garraf Natural Park with the Gravel bike&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and rugged terrain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38142340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sakya Tashi Ling Monastery – Castelldefels Beach loop from Castelldefels&lt;/a&gt;, which also leads through the natural park.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the gravel bike routes around Castelldefels are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31357140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat River Footbridge – Llobregat River Park loop from Castelldefels&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38142340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sakya Tashi Ling Monastery – Castelldefels Beach loop from Castelldefels&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31089249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Rocky Cliff – Lost Hermitage loop from Castelldefels&lt;/a&gt; for another challenging circular ride.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the gravel bike trails?
A: Many routes offer opportunities to see notable landmarks and natural features. Within the Garraf Natural Park, you can visit the &lt;strong&gt;Sakya Tashi Ling Buddhist monastery&lt;/strong&gt;, housed in the Palau Novella. Other highlights in the broader area include various summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2724790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morella Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2852847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de la Mola summit (534 m)&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Riera Caves&lt;/a&gt;. Castelldefels Castle itself offers historical interest and panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Castelldefels?
A: Castelldefels is suitable for gravel biking year-round due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Even winter can be a good time, with cooler, often dry conditions, though some trails might be affected by rain. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended.

Q: What kind of equipment or tires are recommended for gravel biking in Castelldefels?
A: Given the often rocky and dry terrain, especially within the Garraf Natural Park, robust tires are highly recommended for optimal riding. A well-maintained gravel bike with appropriate gearing for climbs will enhance your experience, particularly on the more challenging routes.

Q: How can I access the gravel trails using public transport?
A: Castelldefels is well-connected by public transport, making it accessible from Barcelona and surrounding areas. You can reach Castelldefels by train (Rodalies de Catalunya R2 Sud line) or bus. From the town, many routes are directly accessible, or you can take a short local bus ride to trailheads. Always check local transport schedules for bike carriage policies.

Q: Where can I park my car when heading out for a gravel ride?
A: Castelldefels offers various parking options, particularly near the beach and town center. When planning your ride, look for public parking areas or designated spots close to your chosen trailhead. Some routes, like those starting from Platja de Castelldefels, will have parking facilities nearby.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about the trails in Castelldefels?
A: The gravel biking routes in Castelldefels are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from stunning coastal views to the rugged beauty of the Garraf Natural Park, and the variety of routes catering to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance gravel routes for extended rides?
A: Yes, Castelldefels serves as a starting point for several longer gravel routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e680631082/coast-and-garraf-natural-park-with-the-gravel-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coast and Garraf Natural Park with the Gravel bike&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 62.8 km loop. You can also connect to the wider Penedès area for even more extended rides through rural areas and vineyards, offering varied terrain for longer adventures.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views on the routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes, especially those climbing into the Garraf Natural Park, offer breathtaking panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and the lush Catalan landscape. Castelldefels Castle itself, perched atop a hill, provides excellent vistas. Specific highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6393895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Ramon&lt;/a&gt; are known for their scenic outlooks.
tips: Güell commissioned Gaudí to build a building that had to meet several requirements: to serve as a family home, accommodation for guests, a chapel and cellars for wine production.

The hermitage is found in a document from 1375, and it is stated that there were already hermits. Tradition says that it was built because a cross with the image of the Trinity was found in those places. The current chapel is of modern construction, since at the end of the 18th century the roof collapsed and it had to be rebuilt, expanding at the same time.
It is a small hermitage that is located to the east of Sitges, on top of a mountain that forms Punta de la Ferrosa, it can be reached from a path that leaves the N-246 road. It has an admirable situation since it can be seen from the tip of the Llobregat river to the cape of Salou.

The Palau Novella or Garraf Buddhist Monastery is a former Indian house that today is the Sakya Tashi Ling Buddhist monastery. It is located in the Garraf Natural Park, in the Plana Novella urbanization, in the municipality of Olivella.

Of neo-Gothic and modernist style, it was built in the 19th century by Pere Domènech i Grau, an Indian who returned from Cuba after making his fortune there. Years later, the owner, burdened by debt, sold the palace to a community of Augustinian religious, and in 1996 it was acquired by a Buddhist community who restored it and created a museum. The monastery can be visited, more information at https://monjesbudistas.org/
Gravel Highway: High quality track through the Garraf Natural Park, 100% gravel. In Begues you can fill the bottles of water in different sources. mountain biking friendly :)
Good place to stop to rest with terraces and benches.
